perturbation.original_text: 1. Confidential Information. a) “Confidential Information” means all information both tangible and intangible of a Disclosing Party which relates, respectively, to the above identified su bject matter, including, but not limited to, trade secrets, business and technical information and data, disclosed orally, visually, in writing, electronic media or by any other means, and that is marked in accordance with this Section 1.
perturbation.changed_text: 1. Confidential Information. a) “Confidential Information” means only tangible information of a Disclosing Party which relates, respectively, to the above identified su bject matter, including, but not limited to, trade secrets, business and technical information and data, disclosed in writing, and that is marked in accordance with this Section 1.
perturbation.explanation: By limiting “Confidential Information” to only tangible information disclosed in writing, this contradicts the later sections which discuss oral and visual communication also being treated as confidential, as explained in Section 1(c). The modified definition creates ambiguity about the status of non-written disclosures.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) With respect to any oral or visual communication or other intangible information which a Disclosing Party would like to be treated as Confidential Information under this Agreement, the Disclosing Party shall notify Recipient of such fact at the time of disclosure and within fiftee n (15) days thereafter, Disclosing Party shall send Recipient a written memorandum outlining the information deemed to be Confidential Information. Such memorandum shall be marked “Confidential.”
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits "Confidential Information" to only tangible information disclosed in writing. The contradicted text, Section 1(c), explicitly discusses oral or visual communication being treated as Confidential Information. This creates a direct conflict because the changed text excludes what the contradicted text includes, making the agreement contradictory and therefore triggering a 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-Disclosure. A Recipient of Confidential Information under this Agreement shall use the Confidential Information only for the purpose of evaluating a research relationship between the Parties and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using the same degree of care used to protect its own confidentiality or proprietary information of like importance.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-Disclosure. A Recipient of Confidential Information under this Agreement may use the Confidential Information for any purpose they deem necessary and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “only for the purpose of evaluating a research relationship” to “for any purpose they deem necessary,” the recipient can now use the confidential information for purposes beyond the scope of a research relationship. This conflicts with the contract's purpose of evaluating a research relationship, and the degree of care required is changed from 'the same degree of care used to protect its own confidentiality or proprietary information of like importance' to 'a reasonable degree of care'. This introduces contradiction and ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. No Relationship . This Agreement is intended to provide only for the handling and protection of Confidential Information. It shall not be construed as a teaming, joint venture, partnership or other similar arrangement.
perturbation.justification: The original text limited the use of confidential information 'only for the purpose of evaluating a research relationship.' The changed text expands this to 'for any purpose they deem necessary.' This directly contradicts section 7, 'This Agreement is intended to provide only for the handling and protection of Confidential Information', because the recipient is now allowed to use it for any purpose, which may include activities beyond the scope of handling and protection of Confidential Information related to a research relationship. This introduces a clear and direct conflict in the document. The explanation also notes a change in the degree of care required, which also adds ambiguity and potential unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Exclusions . a) “Confidential Information” shall not include information that: i. was publicly known at the time of the Disclosing Party’s communication thereof; Non-disclosure Agreement – v. 1.0 2 ii. becomes publicly known through no fault of Recipient subsequent to the time of Disclosing Party’s communication thereof to Recipient; iii. was in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at the time of Disclosing Party’s communication thereof to Recipient; iv. is developed by Recipient independently of this Agreement without use or reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information; v. is rightfully obtained by Recipient from a third party, provided the Recipient has no reason to believe that such third party was under an obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Exclusions . a) “Confidential Information” shall not include information that: i. was publicly known at the time of the Disclosing Party’s communication thereof; Non-disclosure Agreement – v. 1.0 2 ii. becomes publicly known through the Recipient's fault subsequent to the time of Disclosing Party’s communication thereof to Recipient; iii. was in Recipient’s possession free of any obligation of confidence at the time of Disclosing Party’s communication thereof to Recipient; iv. is developed by Recipient with reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information; v. is rightfully obtained by Recipient from a third party, provided the Recipient has no reason to believe that such third party was under an obligation of confidentiality to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'through no fault of Recipient' to 'through the Recipient's fault' and changing 'independently of this Agreement without use or reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information' to 'with reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information' create a contradiction. If Confidential Information becomes publicly known through the Recipient's fault it shouldn't be excluded. Similary, if it developed with reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, it also shouldn't be excluded. This introduces significant ambiguity about what information is considered confidential.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-Disclosure. A Recipient of Confidential Information under this Agreement may use the Confidential Information for any purpose they deem necessary and shall protect such Confidential Information from disclosure to others, using a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 3 redefines what constitutes 'Confidential Information' by including information that becomes publicly known through the Recipient's fault and information developed with reference to the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information within the exclusions. This directly contradicts Section 2, which states that the Recipient may use the Confidential Information for any purpose and must protect it from disclosure. If information is considered 'Confidential Information' in Section 2, it implies it should be protected. However, the modified Section 3 now excludes information that became public through the Recipient's fault or was developed with reference to confidential information, thereby creating a direct conflict. The change makes it unclear whether the Recipient is obligated to protect information that falls under these new exclusions, directly contradicting the obligation in Section 2.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Duration of Confidentiality. The Recipient shall maintain in confidence and shall not disclose to any person not a party hereto, unless permitted to do so under Section 2, or use or exploit in any way, without the Disclosing Party’s written agreement, any Confidential Information for a period of five (5) years from the date of disclosure of such information, unless such information ceases to be Confidential Information prior to the end of such five -year period through no fault of Recipient, or Recipient an d Disclosing Party enter into a written agreement authorizing same.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Duration of Confidentiality. The Recipient shall maintain in confidence and shall not disclose to any person not a party hereto, unless permitted to do so under Section 2, or use or exploit in any way, without the Disclosing Party’s written agreement, any Confidential Information indefinitely from the date of disclosure of such information, unless such information ceases to be Confidential Information prior to the end of such five -year period through no fault of Recipient, or Recipient an d Disclosing Party enter into a written agreement authorizing same.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the confidentiality period from 'five (5) years' to 'indefinitely' creates a direct contradiction within the same sentence. The phrase 'unless such information ceases to be Confidential Information prior to the end of such five-year period' is now illogical if the confidentiality lasts indefinitely, making the terms confusing and unenforceable.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Duration of Confidentiality. The Recipient shall maintain in confidence and shall not disclose to any person not a party hereto, unless permitted to do so under Section 2, or use or exploit in any way, without the Disclosing Party’s written agreement, any Confidential Information indefinitely from the date of disclosure of such information, unless such information ceases to be Confidential Information prior to the end of such five -year period through no fault of Recipient, or Recipient an d Disclosing Party enter into a written agreement authorizing same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the duration of confidentiality from 'five (5) years' to 'indefinitely'. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, contains a phrase that is now illogical: 'unless such information ceases to be Confidential Information prior to the end of such five-year period'. This phrase directly contradicts the 'indefinitely' duration, creating an in-text contradiction as the sentence now contains conflicting terms regarding the length of confidentiality, making the clause ambiguous and unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 10. Entire A greement . This Agreement (a) is the complete agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements, understanding or discussions with respect to the subject matter hereof; and (b) may not be amended or in any manner modified except by a non-electronic written instrument signed by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. Entire A greement . This Agreement (a) is the complete agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements, understanding or discussions with respect to the subject matter hereof; and (b) may be amended or in any manner modified orally by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.explanation: By changing “may not be amended or in any manner modified except by a non-electronic written instrument” to “may be amended or in any manner modified orally” introduces a contradiction. The original text establishes that all modifications must be written and signed; the new text implies oral agreements can modify the original agreement, leading to uncertainty about the validity of modifications.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. Entire A greement . This Agreement (a) is the complete agreement of the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof and supersedes any prior agreements, understanding or discussions with respect to the subject matter hereof; and (b) may be amended or in any manner modified orally by authorized representatives of both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that amendments must be in a non-electronic written format, while the changed text allows for oral modifications. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, states that modifications can be made orally. This directly contradicts the initial requirement of written modifications, creating a clear and significant inconsistency within the document, making it unclear how the document can be modified.

perturbation.original_text: 15. Term of Agreement . The term of this Agreement will be one (1) year from the Effective Date, unless it is terminated earlier by either party providing the other party with at least thirty (30) days prior written notice, or unless it is extended as mutually agreed upon by the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 15. Term of Agreement . The term of this Agreement will be one (1) year from the Effective Date, and it can only be terminated with mutual agreement. This agreement is automatically renewed every year without need of both Parties approval
perturbation.explanation: Changing the section on the Term of the Agreement by removing the ability to terminate the agreement with 30 day notice, now making it possible only to terminate it with mutual aggrement and added an automatic renewal creates contradiction. The original text allowed unilateral termination with notice; the modified text requires mutual agreement for termination, which is in direct conflict. As well an auto renewal agreement without any of the parties notice creates conficts of law
perturbation.contradicted_text: 15. Term of Agreement . The term of this Agreement will be one (1) year from the Effective Date, and it can only be terminated with mutual agreement. This agreement is automatically renewed every year without need of both Parties approval
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the option for either party to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice, requiring mutual agreement for termination and adding an automatic renewal clause. This directly contradicts the original text's provision for unilateral termination and introduces a conflict regarding the term and termination conditions, hence there is a strong in-text contradiction.
